source_jobs.title: SEO & GEO Analyst
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity A digital marketing agency focused on the financial services vertical is building out its SEO function to cover both traditional search and AI-driven discovery. You'd own the technical and analytical side of SEO across a client portfolio — not just running audits and reporting rankings, but shaping how the team approaches GEO, consent-aware tracking, and scalable workflows. Strong performance here means clients rank better, track cleaner, and see measurable growth in organic traffic within the first two quarters.

What You'll Own

 - Drive organic performance improvements across client websites through technical SEO, on-page optimization, and structured content strategy

 - Build and maintain accurate tracking infrastructure using GA4 and GTM, ensuring data integrity across client accounts

 - Lead GEO implementation efforts, improving client visibility across AI-powered search experiences and answer engines

 - Audit and support Consent Management Platform setups so tracking remains reliable as privacy regulations evolve

 - Develop repeatable processes and documentation that allow the team to scale SEO execution without losing quality

 - Identify and prioritize ranking, traffic, and conversion opportunities across the client portfolio

What We're Looking For

 - 3+ years in an SEO role at a digital marketing agency (agency experience is required)

 - Hands-on proficiency with Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics 4

 - Solid technical SEO foundation: crawl analysis, site architecture, Core Web Vitals, schema

 - Familiarity with Consent Management Platforms and how consent settings affect data collection

 - Systems thinker: you look for ways to turn one-off tasks into repeatable, scalable workflows

Nice to Have

 - Google Data Studio / Looker Studio reporting experience

 - Experience with AI tools like Claude in day-to-day work (Claude Cowork a plus)

 - Working knowledge of GEO and how AI search differs from traditional SERP optimization

Tools and Stack Google Tag Manager, Google Analytics 4, Google Data Studio/Looker Studio, Consent Management Platforms, Claude/AI workflow tools

Details

 - Location: Remote, Latin America

 - Time Zone: US overlap preferred (specific hours not stated)

 - Salary: Competitive — share your expectations when you apply.

 - Type: Full-time

 - Start: Flexible

About the Company A US-based digital marketing agency that works exclusively with community financial institutions, helping them grow organic traffic, manage their digital presence, and navigate emerging AI-driven search channels. The team is lean and specialized, and this role has real influence over how they evolve their SEO service offering.

How It Works:

 1. Apply below — takes 2 minutes.

 2. Within 5 minutes of applying, you'll receive a phone call from our AI hiring assistant for a quick screening.

 3. If you pass the screening, you'll move into an interview with a LatamCent recruiter.

 4. Top candidates are presented to our client for their interview process.



Discover the best SEO/GEO opportunities with LatamCent.com https://latamcent.com/

source_jobs.title: Sr. SEO Analyst
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

We are hiring a Senior SEO Analyst to lead client relationships and deliver outcome-driven SEO strategies for a growing digital agency. This role is equal parts strategic and executional — you will own client accounts end-to-end, from building SEO strategies to measuring business impact and communicating results.

The ideal candidate brings 3+ years of SEO experience, ideally in an agency setting, and is as comfortable running a technical audit as they are leading a client meeting. This role operates on a 32-hour work week with Fridays offline.

This is a hybrid role based in the US, with 2 days per week in-office expected.



Success in the first 3–6 months looks like:

 - Build strong working relationships across your assigned client accounts and demonstrate a clear understanding of each client's goals and business context

 - Deliver high-quality SEO work including technical audits, content strategies, and performance analyses with minimal ramp-up time

 - Lead client meetings confidently and manage next steps independently

 - Establish efficient delegation workflows with Analyst-level team members in ClickUp

 - Contribute meaningfully to at least one internal initiative or process improvement
   
   

Key Responsibilities

 - Build and implement outcome-driven SEO strategies aligned with each client's goals, business context, and available resources

 - Identify and prioritize SEO recommendations with the highest potential business impact

 - Conduct SEO tasks including technical audits, content outlines, competitor analyses, content research, and performance analyses

 - Analyze performance data to surface relevant insights and develop clear client-facing recommendations

 - Lead client meetings, manage follow-ups, and ensure consistent alignment on strategy and progress

 - Solicit and apply client feedback to continuously improve service delivery

 - Collaborate with team members on strategic decisions and efficient workstream management

 - Delegate tasks clearly and effectively to Analysts within ClickUp

 - Stay current on industry developments and contribute to internal knowledge sharing
   
   

Requirements

 - 3+ years of SEO experience, preferably in an agency environment

 - Proficient technical SEO expertise

 - Experience developing and implementing SEO strategies

 - Familiarity with SEO tools such as Screaming Frog, Ahrefs, and Google Search Console

 - Demonstrated experience experimenting with AI tools

 - Strong organizational and multitasking skills with the ability to manage competing priorities

 - Excellent client communication and presentation skills

 - Strong problem-solving and decision-making abilities

 - Attention to detail and ability to anticipate project risks

 - Adaptable and responsive when priorities shift
   
   

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience working within an EOS-run organization

 - Familiarity with ClickUp for project management

 - Experience working in a results-driven, values-based agency culture
   
   

Compensation & Logistics

 - Hybrid role based in the US — 2 days per week in-office required

 - 32-hour work week with Offline Fridays

 - Salary range: $60,000–$78,000 USD annually

 - Quarterly bonus program based on company and individual performance

 - 401k with 3% company match and profit sharing

 - Medical, dental, and vision insurance options

 - Paid vacation
   
   

About Uproer

Uproer is a digital agency built around the belief that Relationships Yield Results. The team operates on a strong set of core values, runs on EOS, and is committed to work-life balance in practice; not just in policy. Employees are given transparency into business financials, encouraged to build personal career growth plans, and supported by a culture where good ideas can come from anyone.

source_jobs.title: Head of Risk
source_jobs.description:
This is a net-new leadership role for a peer-to-peer marketplace scaling fast, built to give Trust & Safety a real seat at the table instead of being a reactive cost center. You'll report straight to the COO and own the function end to end: fraud prevention, identity verification, investigations, and moderation. Success looks like a measurably safer platform within a year, fewer fraud losses, faster incident response, and a T&S team running on real workflows and automation instead of tribal knowledge.

What You'll Own

 - Build and run the Trust & Safety operating model from the ground up: team structure, workflows, tooling, and the roadmap to scale it

 - Drive down fraud losses and platform risk by defining and tracking the metrics that actually move the needle

 - Translate marketplace threats into executive-ready reporting that gets budget and buy-in

 - Partner with Product and Engineering to ship fraud detection, identity verification, and moderation tooling that delivers measurable results, not just features

 - Own high-severity incident response, connecting Legal, Engineering, and Operations so investigations actually change policy and product

 - Evaluate and manage vendor relationships for fraud and identity tools, owning the build-vs-buy calls

 - Set the policy foundation with Legal, keeping it audit-ready and enforceable across the org

What We're Looking For

 - 7+ years in Trust & Safety, Fraud Operations, or Risk Management

 - 5+ years leading people, including managing other managers or senior ICs

 - A track record of building or scaling a Trust & Safety, fraud, or marketplace-integrity function, not just running an existing one

 - Proven ability to turn operational problems into systems and influence cross-functional roadmaps

 - Experience reporting metrics and outcomes directly to executive stakeholders

 - Background in a high-growth marketplace, platform, or consumer internet business

 - Professional fluency in English, C1 minimum, written and spoken

Nice to Have

 - Background in P2P marketplaces, rental platforms, or fintech

 - Hands-on experience with identity verification or fraud tools like Jumio, LexisNexis, TransUnion, or IPQS

 - Experience standing up AI or automation-driven workflows inside a T&S org

Tools and Stack
Zendesk, Snowflake, Jira, Confluence, Slack, plus identity/fraud platforms like Jumio or IPQS

Details

 - Location: Remote, Colombia

 - Time Zone: CT

 - Type: Full-time

About the Company
An established, fast-scaling two-sided marketplace in flexible housing, connecting hundreds of thousands of property owners with renters needing month-plus stays. Profitable, over a decade in business, and recently recognized as a category leader in its space.

source_jobs.title: Sr. Full Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Our client is hiring a Senior Full Stack Software Engineer to join a lean, high-performing engineering team. This is a critical hire. The team is small, fast-moving, and tightly scheduled. There is no room for hand-holding or extended ramp time.

This role requires a true engineering craftsman. Someone who takes ownership, solves problems independently, and chooses the right tools for the job. Java expertise is essential. Frontend experience with TypeScript is important. Angular is a plus but not mandatory.

The engineering team is based in Europe, with leadership in U.S. Mountain and Central time zones. This hire must provide meaningful time overlap with both regions.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, build, and maintain backend services primarily in Java

 - Contribute to frontend development using TypeScript and modern frameworks

 - Own features end-to-end, from architecture to production deployment

 - Troubleshoot and resolve production bugs quickly, especially during U.S. business hours

 - Collaborate closely with Product and Engineering leadership across Europe and the U.S.

 - Participate in code reviews and maintain high engineering standards

 - Contribute to architectural discussions and technical decision-making

 - Work independently in a small team of 4 engineers
   


REQUIREMENTS

 - 7+ years of software engineering experience

 - Strong expertise in Java

 - Solid frontend experience with TypeScript

 - Experience working across the full stack

 - Proven experience in small, fast-paced teams (not exclusively large enterprise environments)

 - Strong ownership mindset with demonstrated examples of independently solving production issues

 - Experience shipping production-grade software in tight deadlines

 - Excellent communication skills

 - English level: C1 or C2

 - Availability to overlap with:
   
   - U.S. Mountain Time
   
   - U.S. Central Time
   
   - European time zones




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE (FIRST 3–6 MONTHS)

 - Independently contributing code within the first few weeks

 - Taking ownership of production issues without requiring close supervision

 - Shipping features that meet quality and timeline expectations

 - Demonstrating proactive communication across time zones

 - Elevating engineering standards rather than requiring onboarding support
   
   


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience with Angular

 - Experience working in AI-driven or recruitment-tech products

 - Exposure to Kubernetes or modern cloud infrastructure

 - Experience in high-growth or VC-backed startup environments

 - Polyglot mindset with ability to adapt across technologies
   


COMPENSATION & LOGISTICS

 - Remote position – must be based in Latin America

 - Full-time role

 - Long-term engagement

 - Time zone overlap required with U.S. and Europe
   
   
   


COMPANY OVERVIEW

Our client is an AI-driven recruitment platform helping companies improve how they screen and evaluate candidates. The company operates with a lean team and high execution standards. Engineering quality, ownership, and speed matter.

This is an opportunity to join a product positioned at the intersection of AI and hiring technology, contributing meaningfully during a high-growth stage.

source_jobs.title: Go Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

SourceFuse is looking for a Go Backend Engineer to join the team building the execution layer of a high-throughput data platform. This is a high-impact, technically demanding role where you will own the core streaming and data processing infrastructure — from Kafka-based ingestion through windowed aggregation, KPI computation, and time-series storage.

You will work on systems that process millions of data points every 15 minutes, where performance, reliability, and correctness are non-negotiable. The ideal candidate brings deep Go expertise, hands-on Kafka experience in high-throughput environments, and the distributed systems mindset to deliver production-grade pipelines independently — with zero REST API surface. Everything here is event-driven, internal, and pipeline-first.

In the first 3–6 months, success means:

 - Own and deliver high-performance Kafka consumers/producers in Go, meeting throughput and latency requirements

 - Implement windowed aggregation, state handling, and idempotent writes to TSDB without requiring daily oversight

 - Build out adapters for DB/API/SFTP ingestion with clean retry and DLQ strategies

 - Contribute to observability infrastructure and establish profiling baselines

 - Deliver against project milestones in a fast-paced Silicon Valley startup environment

 - Integrate smoothly with a cross-functional, globally distributed team (US + Asia time zones)




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build high-performance Kafka consumers and producers in Go for a high-throughput data platform

 - Implement windowed aggregation and state handling for real-time streaming pipelines

 - Design and implement retry logic and dead letter queue (DLQ) strategies to ensure data reliability

 - Ensure idempotent writes to time-series databases (TSDB — cloud-native, e.g. ADX, Snowflake)

 - Build adapters for DB, API, and SFTP-based data ingestion

 - Implement dynamic configuration management using CRD watcher patterns

 - Write production-grade, secure code following performance profiling and optimization best practices

 - Work within a fully cloud-native Kubernetes environment (Azure-first, expanding to AWS/GCP)

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams across different cultures, organizations, and time zones


REQUIREMENTS

 - 4–8 years of overall backend engineering experience

 - 3+ years of hands-on Go development in production environments

 - Strong knowledge of Go concurrency patterns: goroutines, channels, and sync primitives

 - Kafka integration experience in high-throughput, event-driven distributed systems (Avro/REST schema, performance tuning)

 - Docker and Kubernetes proficiency — cloud-managed environments (Azure, AWS, or GCP)

 - Experience building and maintaining data pipelines and event-driven architectures — not REST API-only profiles

 - Observability instrumentation (logging, metrics, tracing)

 - Secure coding practices and performance profiling experience

 - Strong distributed systems fundamentals

 - Experience working with high-throughput, high-volume data at scale (e.g. IoT, fintech, adtech, large-scale monitoring, or similar domains)

 - Excellent communication skills in English — able to articulate technical decisions clearly under pressure

 - Self-directed and delivery-oriented; able to operate in fast-paced startup environments with shifting requirements

 - English fluency: C1 or higher (assessed under realistic working conditions)

 - Must overlap with PST (Pacific Standard Time) working hours


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience with large-scale monitoring/observability platforms or high-throughput data systems (highly preferred)

 - Time-series database experience — ADX (Azure Data Explorer) or Snowflake (highly preferred)

 - Event-driven architecture depth (highly preferred)

 - Experience with streaming systems (Flink, Spark Streaming, or similar)

 - Familiarity with telecom or IoT network data semantics

 - Experience in platform or SaaS control plane

source_jobs.title: Director, AI & Cloud Services Sales [West Coast]
source_jobs.description:
DIRECTOR, AI & CLOUD SERVICES SALES




THE OPPORTUNITY

A global cloud-native services firm is hiring its first dedicated AI and cloud services seller for California, and wants one person to own the territory end to end. You will land net-new enterprise logos, sell AI and cloud transformation work (not staff augmentation) into CIOs, CDOs, and Chief AI Officers, and report straight to the CEO and founders. Success at 90 days is a qualified pipeline you built from your own network; at 12 months, closed multi-million-dollar engagements and a territory that runs on your name. Base is steady, commission is uncapped, and the comp scales hard if you close.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Build the California go-to-market from zero and carry the net-new logo quota yourself

 - Run full-cycle deals from first conversation through proposal, negotiation, and signature

 - Activate your existing CIO/CDO/Chief AI Officer relationships from day one

 - Translate client business problems into AI-led, outcome-based proposals instead of headcount pitches

 - Co-sell with AWS, Databricks, and Snowflake field teams to source and accelerate deals

 - Represent the company at AWS, AI, and industry events and build a point of view that pulls inbound


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 7+ years in enterprise technology sales (services, SaaS, or consulting); 10+ preferred

 - Proven close history on $1M-$5M+ deals in the US market

 - An existing relationship book in CIO/CDO/Chief AI Officer or senior enterprise buyer communities you can activate now

 - Real AI and modern data fluency: credible on GenAI, Databricks, Snowflake, and AWS without pre-sales backup

 - Based in California

 - Comfortable as a solo hunter reporting directly to founders, with no team beneath you to start


NICE TO HAVE

 - Databricks, Snowflake, or AWS partner co-sell experience

 - Vertical depth in financial services, healthcare, or mid-market SaaS

 - A track record of a company that pivoted into AI, where you led the new motion


TOOLS AND STACK

AI/cloud services portfolio: GenAI and agentic systems, application and platform modernization, cloud data platforms and MLOps, managed services. AWS-led hyperscaler co-sell, Databricks and Snowflake partnerships.




DETAILS

 - Location: California, US (remote, with travel for events and customers)

 - Salary: $145k -165k base, uncapped commission (realistic path to 200k+) First Year, with compounding gains year 2-5.

 - Type: Full-time

 - Start: ASAP


ABOUT THE COMPANY

A cloud-native technology services firm with 19+ years in market and 550+ people globally, helping enterprises run AI and cloud transformation. They sell as a strategic transformation partner, not a vendor, into mid-market and enterprise accounts across financial services, healthcare, and SaaS.

source_jobs.title: SDR
source_jobs.description:
We are hiring a Sales Development Representative (SDR) to generate new outbound opportunities across construction and software-enabled commercial services. This role sits at the very top of the funnel and is responsible for initiating conversations, qualifying early interest, and building a predictable outbound pipeline.

You will be the first point of contact for many prospects. Success in this role requires strong outbound discipline, comfort with cold calling, and the ability to quickly identify which opportunities should advance to the sales team.

This is a hands-on, high-activity role designed for someone who wants to grow in sales while helping build a scalable outbound motion.




WHAT YOU’LL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR

 - Execute high-volume outbound outreach, including 150+ cold calls per day, plus email, SMS, and LinkedIn messaging

 - Introduce construction and software-enabled service solutions to commercial prospects across franchise, retail, QSR, and enterprise environments

 - Identify and engage general contractors, property owners, franchise operators, and commercial decision-makers

 - Run top-of-funnel conversations to generate interest and qualify basic fit before routing opportunities to the sales team

 - Build and maintain outbound prospect lists aligned with the ideal customer profile

 - Research companies, markets, and decision-makers to uncover new opportunities

 - Log activity, notes, and lead status accurately in the CRM

 - Follow up consistently with warm prospects to convert interest into booked meetings

 - Track daily and weekly activity metrics and contribute feedback to outreach scripts and strategy

 - Partner closely with sales to ensure clean handoffs and aligned pipeline priorities


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 1 to 2 years of experience in outbound sales, prospecting, or SDR roles

 - Prior exposure to B2B, construction, commercial services, or SaaS environments is a plus

 - Strong comfort with cold calling and multi-channel outreach at high volume

 - Excellent English communication skills, written and verbal (C1 or C2 required)

 - Ability to research accounts, identify decision-makers, and understand commercial buying contexts

 - Highly organized with strong follow-through on outreach and follow-ups

 - Self-motivated, coachable, and comfortable operating in a fast-paced growth environment


COMPENSATION

2000 USD base + commissions




COMPANY OVERVIEW

Our client is a fast-growing, technology-enabled commercial services company operating within a large, fragmented U.S. market. They combine experienced field teams with modern systems to deliver high-quality service for commercial and construction clients nationwide.

The company is actively scaling its go-to-market team and investing in outbound sales to support continued expansion.



Job description created by latamcent.com http://latamcent.com, a nearshore staffing agency.

source_jobs.title: IT & Compliance Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for an experienced IT & Compliance Specialist to own and scale our security compliance program while managing internal IT operations. You'll work closely with Engineering, Operations, and Leadership, supported by the CTO and a part-time consultant with deep SOC 2 and MDM expertise, but day-to-day ownership is yours.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



SOC 2 COMPLIANCE (PRIMARY PRIORITY)

 - Own and maintain the SOC 2 compliance program: continuous monitoring, audit readiness, evidence collection, and control tracking

 - Administer Vanta as the primary compliance automation platform, it needs work, and cleaning it up is part of the job

 - Conduct regular access reviews and ensure documentation is complete and accurate

 - Facilitate tabletop exercises covering scenarios like AWS outages, cyber incidents, and availability failures

 - Manage vendor relationships: Vanta contract, pentester engagements, and third-party security assessments

 - Develop, maintain, and improve information security policies, procedures, and documentation

Device Fleet & MDM

 - Own corporate device management across a mixed fleet of macOS, Linux, and Windows machines

 - Implement centralized MDM controls: encryption, anti-malware, endpoint detection, and remote management

 - Establish and enforce a BYOD policy for employees and contractors using personal hardware

 - Build repeatable onboarding and offboarding processes so device and access controls are never an afterthought

Cloud & AWS Compliance

 - Maintain AWS security hygiene: IAM roles, Identity Center, GuardDuty, AWS Config, and access reviews

 - Identify and remediate overly permissive roles, stale credentials, and misconfigured controls

 - Collaborate with the technical team in Caxias do Sul to resolve vulnerabilities and apply patches

 - Support cloud-related evidence collection for SOC 2 controls

IT Operations & Access Management

 - Own IT onboarding and offboarding: provisioning, deprovisioning, and access controls

 - Manage access across Google Workspace, Slack, GitHub, Rippling, AWS Identity Center, and other core tools

 - Serve as the primary internal IT resource and respond to urgent issues as they arise




REQUIREMENTS

 - 3+ years in IT, Security, Compliance, or related roles within a SaaS or high-growth tech environment

 - Proven hands-on experience managing SOC 2 Type I and/or Type II programs

 - Direct experience with Vanta, including interpreting findings and driving remediation

 - Experience managing devices across macOS, Linux, and Windows using MDM tools such as JumpCloud, Jamf, Kandji, or similar

 - Working knowledge of AWS security: IAM, Identity Center, GuardDuty, AWS Config, and access best practices

 - Strong understanding of identity and access management, MFA, encryption, endpoint security, and audit controls

 - C1+ English for daily collaboration with the US-based CTO and leadership

 - Execution-focused: the CTO and consultant will support you, but the day-to-day needs someone who follows through without being managed step by ste


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Scripting experience (Python preferred) for automating compliance checks or IT workflows

 - Experience with AI tools such as Claude or ChatGPT applied to compliance or IT operations

 - Familiarity with our stack: Google Workspace, Slack, GitHub, Rippling, Linear, AWS Identity Center

 - Experience owning vendor contracts or working with pentesters and external assessors

 - Prior experience working with or at a US-headquartered company with a Brazilian engineering team

 - Certifications such as CompTIA Security+, AWS Security, or SOC 2-related credentials are a plus




SUCCESS IN THE FIRST 3–6 MONTHS

First 60 Days

 - Every open finding in Vanta has a clear owner and remediation timeline

 - The device fleet is fully inventoried: what's enrolled, what's not, what needs to happen

 - Working relationships built with the technical team in Caxias do Sul

 - Top AWS hygi

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity

A fast-growing AI platform serving enterprise customers is building out its deployment team to own the technical side of client rollouts end-to-end. You'll be the primary technical contact for major accounts, taking AI-powered solutions from configuration to production and keeping them running at scale.

This is not a support role. It's not pre-sales. It's a heavy engineering function with full client ownership — the kind of work where you're in architecture discussions with a CTO one hour and debugging a production incident the next. If you move fast, think in systems, and want your name on the accounts you deploy, this is where that happens.



What You'll Own

 - Lead the full deployment lifecycle for enterprise integrations — from initial client configuration through production go-live and ongoing reliability.

 - Serve as the primary technical contact for a portfolio of enterprise clients: run architecture discussions, own escalations, and keep systems healthy.

 - Build and maintain monitoring infrastructure, dashboards, and alerting systems that surface issues before they become client problems.

 - Own backend and API integrations across enterprise systems, ensuring secure and stable production operation.

 - Translate client requirements into scalable solutions within platform constraints, and bring patterns back to product and engineering to close gaps.

 - Drive a data-informed approach to deployments through logging and analytics systems that enable proactive optimization.

 

What We're Looking For

 - 2–5 years in a client-facing technical role — solutions engineering, technical consulting, or deployment/implementation. You've owned external relationships, not just supported them.

 - Python in production — backend services, APIs, automation. Scripting-only experience is not sufficient.

 - Docker for containerized deployments. Kubernetes experience is a strong plus.

 - REST API integrations at enterprise scale — day one capability, no ramp-up needed.

 - Comfortable owning complex enterprise stakeholder relationships independently — from architecture decisions to escalation handling, without hand-holding.

 - AI-assisted development mindset — you use AI tooling to move faster and expect it to handle the majority of your code.

 - Native-level English fluency — written and verbal. You'll interface directly with CTOs and senior executives.

 - PST full business hours availability — based in LATAM, full overlap required.

Nice to Have

 - Background in AI, NLP, or conversational systems

 - Experience with VoIP, SIP/RTP, CCaaS, or CPaaS platforms

 - Experience at a contact center technology or voice infrastructure company

 - Exposure to enterprise backend service architecture and multi-system integrations

Tools and Stack

Python, Docker, Kubernetes, SIP/RTP, REST APIs & monitoring/observability tooling (*Specifics TBD by client stack)

Details

 - Location: Remote, Latin America

 - Time Zone: PST overlap required (full business hours)

 - Salary: Competitive — share your expectations when you apply.

 - Type: Contract, full-time

 - Start: ASAP

About the Company

An enterprise AI platform that automates customer service interactions at scale, deployed across global enterprise accounts handling tens of thousands of calls daily. The company is well-funded, growing fast, and expanding its deployment team to support a rapidly scaling client base.

How It Works:

 1. Apply below — takes 2 minutes.

 2. Within 5 minutes of applying, you'll receive a phone call from our AI hiring assistant for a quick screening.

 3. If you pass the screening, you'll move into an interview with a LatamCent recruiter.

 4. Top candidates are presented to our client for their interview process.

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
CUSTOMER SUCCESS MANAGER




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are hiring a Customer Success Manager to support a fast-growing SaaS company serving Shopify merchants. This is a high-impact, client-facing role where you'll manage a large portfolio of customers, helping them maximize product adoption, improve retention strategies, and achieve long-term success.

You'll become a trusted advisor to customers while partnering closely with Product and Engineering teams to improve the customer experience and influence product development. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working in fast-paced startup environments, embraces ambiguity, and takes ownership without waiting for direction.

This is a fully remote opportunity for professionals based in Latin America with availability to work during US Pacific Time business hours.

 
 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage a portfolio of 100+ customer accounts, providing strategic guidance and day-to-day support.

 - Build strong relationships with customers and serve as their primary point of contact.

 - Drive customer adoption, engagement, retention, and overall account health.

 - Lead proactive customer check-ins, business reviews, and success planning.

 - Respond quickly to customer questions and coordinate issue resolution across internal teams.

 - Partner closely with Product and Engineering by communicating customer feedback, reporting bugs, and identifying product improvement opportunities.

 - Help improve onboarding experiences, customer education, and success processes as the company continues to scale.

 - Maintain accurate customer records and ensure timely follow-up on all customer interactions.

 


REQUIREMENTS

 - 3+ years of experience in Customer Success, Account Management, Technical Account Management, or a similar customer-facing SaaS role.

 - Experience managing a high-volume portfolio of customer accounts.

 - Strong relationship-building and stakeholder management skills.

 - Excellent written and verbal English communication skills (C1 or C2).

 - Comfortable working in fast-moving startup environments with evolving processes.

 - Strong organizational and time management skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.

 - Technical curiosity and the ability to troubleshoot basic product or integration issues.

 - Familiarity with Shopify or the eCommerce ecosystem.

 - Availability to work during US Pacific Time business hours.

 


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Previous startup experience, particularly with early-stage or high-growth companies.

 - Experience supporting Shopify merchants or eCommerce brands.

 - Familiarity with loyalty, retention, or customer engagement platforms.

 - Basic knowledge of HTML, CSS, APIs, or technical troubleshooting.

 - Experience collaborating with Product and Engineering teams to improve customer outcomes.

 


SUCCESS IN THE FIRST 3 TO 6 MONTHS

 - Successfully onboard and independently manage a portfolio of 100+ customer accounts.

 - Build trusted relationships with key customers while maintaining high customer satisfaction.

 - Improve product adoption and customer engagement across your account portfolio.

 - Become a reliable cross-functional partner by providing valuable customer insights to Product and Engineering.

 - Contribute to scalable customer success processes that support the company's continued growth.

 


COMPENSATION & LOGISTICS

 - Employment Type: Full-time

 - Location: Remote (Latin America)

 - Schedule: Full-time with working hours aligned to US Pacific Time

 - Compensation: USD $3000 to $4000 a month, depending on experience

 
 


COMPANY OVERVIEW

Our client is a fast-growing SaaS company building products for the Shopify ecosystem. With a customer-first mindset and a collaborative startup culture, the team is focused on helping eCommerce brands grow through innovative technology and exceptional customer experiences. This is an opportunity to join an early-stage company where you'l

source_jobs.title: Legal Counsel
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We are hiring a Legal Counsel to own the commercial and contract workflows that support revenue growth, customer onboarding, and operational compliance.

This role sits at the intersection of sales operations, legal operations, contract management, and compliance. You will manage the full contract lifecycle, support commercial negotiations, coordinate due diligence processes, and ensure agreements move efficiently from proposal through signature, implementation, and renewal.

The ideal candidate combines strong contract management experience with commercial awareness. You should be comfortable reviewing and negotiating SaaS agreements, working directly with customers, and building processes that improve visibility and execution in a fast-growing startup environment.

This is a highly cross-functional role that works closely with leadership, operations, finance, engineering, and external counsel.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage the full contract lifecycle, from drafting and review through execution, renewal, and ongoing compliance

 - Draft, review, and redline commercial agreements, including MSAs, NDAs, Order Forms, pilot agreements, and related documents

 - Support customer contract negotiations while balancing business objectives and risk management

 - Guide prospects through proposals and commercial discussions, ensuring momentum throughout the sales process

 - Serve as the primary internal point of contact for contract-related questions and documentation

 - Complete client security questionnaires, vendor due diligence requests, and compliance-related documentation

 - Support SOC 2 reviews, compliance initiatives, and customer procurement processes

 - Maintain contract repositories, templates, playbooks, and documentation standards

 - Build and improve operational systems, trackers, dashboards, and workflows to increase visibility and efficiency

 - Partner with finance on invoicing, accounts receivable tracking, collections follow-up, and revenue operations processes

 - Develop commercial proposals, business case summaries, and supporting materials for customer-facing opportunities

 - Collaborate with founders, operations, finance, engineering, and external counsel to move deals forward efficiently


REQUIREMENTS

 - 8+ years of experience in Legal Operations, Sales Operations, Contract Management, Commercial Operations, or a related role

 - Experience reviewing, redlining, and negotiating commercial agreements

 - Experience negotiating contracts with enterprise customers

 - Strong understanding of SaaS contracts, including MSAs, NDAs, Order Forms, and service agreements

 - Familiarity with key contract concepts such as liability, indemnification, renewals, data protection, and commercial terms

 - Experience managing multiple contracts, stakeholders, and deadlines simultaneously

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English

 - Strong organizational skills and exceptional attention to detail

 - Ability to simplify complex contractual concepts for business stakeholders

 - Comfortable working directly with customers and external partners

 - Ability to operate independently in a fast-paced startup environment

 - Strong ownership mindset and proactive problem-solving abilities

 - English proficiency: C2




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Law degree or legal background

 - Experience supporting B2B SaaS companies

 - Familiarity with SOC 2 compliance requirements and vendor due diligence processes

 - Experience completing security questionnaires and procurement documentation

 - Exposure to revenue operations, deal desk, or sales enablement functions

 - Experience supporting invoicing, billing, collections, or accounts receivable processes

 - Experience creating contract playbooks, clause libraries, and standardized workflows

 - Previous startup or early-stage company experience


SUCCESS METRICS (FIRST 3-6 MONTHS)

 - Take ownership of the contract lifecycle and redu

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Main Street AI Advisors is hiring a full-time AI Engineer to design, build, and deliver practical AI solutions for small and midsize businesses across the United States.

This is a client-facing, delivery-focused role where you'll own the entire implementation lifecycle for a portfolio of customers. You'll lead client meetings, manage project timelines, build AI-powered automations, deploy production-ready solutions, and ensure projects stay on schedule and within scope.

We're looking for an engineer who combines strong technical skills with excellent communication and project ownership. Success in this role comes from shipping reliable solutions, managing client expectations, and solving business problems with practical AI instead of unnecessary complexity.

This is a fully remote position open to professionals across Latin America who can maintain regular overlap with US Pacific Time.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


CLIENT SUCCESS & COMMUNICATION

 - Serve as the primary technical contact for assigned client accounts.

 - Lead weekly client meetings, establish priorities, document decisions, and communicate next steps.

 - Build trusted relationships with business stakeholders through proactive, transparent communication.

 - Identify project risks early and communicate solutions before issues escalate.

 - Collaborate with company leadership during client onboarding, strategic planning, and key project milestones.


PROJECT OWNERSHIP

 - Manage multiple client engagements simultaneously.

 - Deliver projects on time while staying within agreed scope and allocated hours.

 - Identify out-of-scope requests and coordinate expansion opportunities with leadership.

 - Maintain accurate project documentation, timelines, task status, and time tracking.

 - Prioritize practical, maintainable solutions over unnecessary complexity.


TECHNICAL DELIVERY

 - Design, build, test, and deploy AI agents and workflow automations.

 - Develop integrations between AI systems and client applications using APIs.

 - Build automation workflows using platforms such as Make.com, n8n, Zapier, Lindy.ai, or similar tools.

 - Write JavaScript and Python to support integrations, business logic, and custom workflows.

 - Configure and manage cloud infrastructure required for client deployments.

 - Perform end-to-end testing, debugging, monitoring, and error handling.

 - Produce technical documentation for internal teams and user-friendly documentation for clients.

 - Estimate ongoing infrastructure, API, cloud, and AI model costs for client solutions.

 - Select appropriate AI models and architectures with cost, performance, and reliability in mind.

 - Research client technology stacks and integration capabilities before implementation.

 - Support internal automation and AI initiatives as bandwidth allows.

 


REQUIREMENTS

 - 3+ years of experience delivering software, automation, AI, or integration projects.

 - Demonstrated experience owning projects from discovery through deployment.

 - Experience working directly with clients or business stakeholders.

 - Strong written and verbal English communication skills (C1 or C2).

 - Advanced JavaScript and JSON experience.

 - Working knowledge of Python for scripting and automation.

 - Experience building REST API integrations, authentication flows, OAuth, and webhooks.

 - Hands-on experience integrating LLMs such as OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, or similar models.

 - Experience with automation platforms including Make.com, n8n, Zapier, Lindy.ai, or comparable tools.

 - Experience deploying and managing cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, or similar).

 - Excellent project management, organization, and time management skills.

 - Ability to manage multiple priorities while maintaining high-quality client communication.

 - Availability to overlap several hours with US Pacific Time.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Previous consulting, agency, or professional services experie

source_jobs.title: Creator & Community Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

FlipaClip is looking for a Creator & Community Manager to build and scale relationships with the creators who make our community thrive. You'll identify talented animators, develop long-term creator partnerships, manage creator programs, and help shape community initiatives that inspire millions of animation enthusiasts worldwide.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone early in their career who is passionate about animation, creator communities, and influencer partnerships. You'll work closely with Growth, Product, and Social teams while owning day-to-day creator engagement and helping evolve FlipaClip's global creator ecosystem.

This is a remote position open to candidates across Latin America who can collaborate effectively with a distributed team.

 
 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Identify and recruit animation creators across YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, and other relevant platforms.

 - Evaluate creators using internal qualification frameworks and maintain an organized creator database.

 - Own creator outreach, relationship management, and ongoing communications.

 - Schedule, host, and document Creator Talks, sharing insights with internal stakeholders.

 - Coordinate creator campaigns from briefing through content delivery and publication.

 - Review creator content to ensure brand quality and campaign alignment.

 - Monitor campaign performance using tracking links, promotional codes, and reporting dashboards.

 - Identify high-performing creators and recommend opportunities to expand successful partnerships.

 - Collaborate with Growth, Product, and Social teams to share creator feedback and generate new content opportunities.

 - Help plan and launch community initiatives such as contests, challenges, and digital creator events.

 
 


REQUIREMENTS

 - 1 to 2 years of experience in creator partnerships, influencer marketing, community management, content marketing, or a related field.

 - Strong passion for animation and creator culture.

 - Familiarity with animation communities across YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, and other social platforms.

 - Excellent relationship-building and communication skills.

 - C1 or C2 English proficiency, both written and spoken.

 - Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple creator relationships simultaneously.

 - Comfortable working with spreadsheets and interpreting basic performance metrics.

 - Self-motivated and able to work independently in a remote environment.

 
 


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Personal experience creating animation, illustration, or digital content.

 - Experience managing creator or influencer programs.

 - Basic video editing or content production skills.

 - Additional language proficiency beyond English.

 - Experience organizing online communities, contests, or virtual events.

 
 


SUCCESS INDICATORS (FIRST 3 TO 6 MONTHS)

First 30 Days

 - Become fully familiar with FlipaClip's creator program, workflows, and creator database.

 - Begin independently managing Creator Talks.

 - Build relationships with key creators and internal stakeholders.

By 90 Days

 - Successfully manage an active portfolio of creator partnerships.

 - Establish a consistent Creator Talks cadence.

 - Demonstrate an understanding of campaign performance and creator impact.

By 6 Months

 - Build a healthy pipeline of new creator partnerships.

 - Launch or prepare the company's next creator-focused community initiative, such as a contest or challenge.

 - Become the primary owner of the creator community program.

 
 


COMPENSATION & LOGISTICS

 - Remote position based in Latin America.

 - Full-time or contract opportunity.

 - Time zone: Flexible, with overlap required with the broader Growth team.

 
 


COMPANY OVERVIEW

FlipaClip, part of Visual Blasters, is the world's leading mobile animation app, empowering millions of creators to produce frame-by-frame animations from anywhere. The company continues to grow through its creator-first ap

source_jobs.title: Sr. Mobile Product Delivery Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We are hiring a Senior Mobile Product Delivery Manager to own how our mobile product gets planned, prioritized, and shipped across iOS and Android.

This role sits at the center of product delivery, ensuring work moves efficiently from concept to release. You will own sequencing, prioritization, dependency management, and delivery timelines while partnering closely with Engineering, Growth, Data, Product, and Leadership.

You will play a key role in shaping the product roadmap, balancing user needs, business impact, and technical complexity to determine what gets built next. This is a senior-level position with significant influence on product direction and execution.

You will report directly to the CEO and operate as a peer to the Engineering Lead, Head of Growth, and Data Lead.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own end-to-end delivery planning across mobile product initiatives.

 - Create and maintain prioritized roadmaps, release plans, and delivery timelines.

 - Drive sequencing, prioritization, work-in-progress management, and dependency resolution.

 - Coordinate execution across engineering, growth, analytics, QA, and leadership stakeholders.

 - Translate strategic goals into actionable plans and clear execution priorities.

 - Research features opportunities, customer feedback, and product signals to inform roadmap decisions.

 - Evaluate effort versus impact to support prioritization and investment decisions.

 - Facilitate sprint planning, backlog management, release coordination, and delivery reviews.

 - Identify delivery risks early and drive alignment on mitigation strategies.

 - Lead discussions that unblock teams and accelerate decision-making.

 - Ensure mobile, backend, analytics, and growth initiatives remain aligned throughout execution.

 - Establish operational processes that improve predictability, velocity, and release quality.


REQUIREMENTS

 - 3+ years of experience as a Technical Program Manager, Technical Producer, Delivery Lead, or similar role supporting consumer mobile applications.

 - Proven experience shipping products on both iOS and Android.

 - Experience working on consumer-facing applications with significant user bases.

 - Strong background in agile delivery practices, including sprint planning, backlog grooming, release management, and dependency tracking.

 - Hands-on experience coordinating work across mobile, backend, analytics, QA, and product teams.

 - Advanced proficiency with Jira and agile project management workflows.

 - Strong product judgment with experience contributing to discovery, requirements definition, and prioritization decisions.

 - Ability to independently drive initiatives from planning through release.

 - Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.

 - Comfortable influencing senior leaders and making prioritization recommendations.

 - English proficiency: C2 (written and verbal).

 - Availability to work in America time zones.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience shipping creative tools, gaming products, animation software, or children's and family applications.

 - Experience working in experimentation-driven environments using tools such as Amplitude and A/B testing frameworks.

 - Experience managing external platforms or strategic partner relationships.

 - Spanish proficiency..
   


SUCCESS INDICATORS (FIRST 3–6 MONTHS)


WITHIN 3 MONTHS

 - Develop a clear understanding of the product roadmap, delivery process, and team structure.

 - Establish visibility into active initiatives, dependencies, and delivery risks.

 - Improve planning and prioritization processes across teams.

 - Create reliable release planning and execution workflows.


WITHIN 6 MONTHS

 - Own roadmap execution across major product initiatives.

 - Improve delivery predictability, team alignment, and release velocity.

 - Drive prioritization decisions that balance business impact, technical effort, and customer value.

 - Become a trusted strategic

source_jobs.title: Paid Media Search Analyst
source_jobs.description:
We are hiring a Paid Media Search Analyst to support the execution and optimization of outcome-driven paid search campaigns across multiple client accounts. This role is hands-on and execution-focused, sitting at the intersection of campaign production, performance analysis, and client support.

You will work closely with senior paid media strategists to manage day-to-day campaign operations, surface insights, and deliver consistent, high-quality outputs that directly impact client growth.

This role is ideal for someone early in their paid media career who wants to strengthen their analytical skills, develop disciplined execution habits, and grow into a Paid Media Strategist over time.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


CLIENT & TEAM COLLABORATION

 - Support client accounts with an owner’s mindset across all paid search work
   

 - Maintain high standards for communication, responsiveness, and follow-through
   

 - Incorporate client and strategist feedback to continuously improve execution
   

 - Ensure all campaign work aligns with client goals and outcome-driven strategies
   


CAMPAIGN PRODUCTION & EXECUTION

 - Build, manage, and optimize paid search campaigns across Google Ads and Microsoft Ads
   

 - Execute keyword research, ad copy testing, bid adjustments, and audience targeting
   

 - Monitor performance daily to identify issues, risks, and optimization opportunities
   

 - Maintain accurate task and time tracking while meeting delivery expectations
   

 - Follow internal processes, documentation, and quality standards consistently
   


ANALYSIS & OPTIMIZATION

 - Analyze campaign performance to generate clear insights and recommendations
   

 - Support budget pacing, forecasting, and efficiency improvements
   

 - Assist in preparing performance reports and client-ready insights
   

 - Collaborate with strategists on testing roadmaps and optimization plans
   


GROWTH & CONTRIBUTION

 - Participate in client meetings and support follow-up action items
   

 - Stay current on paid media platform updates and industry best practices
   

 - Contribute to internal documentation and process improvements
   

 - Actively manage personal skill development and career growth goals
   


REQUIREMENTS


HARD SKILLS

 - 2+ years of paid media or digital marketing experience, preferably in an agency environment
   

 - Hands-on experience managing Google Ads campaigns
   

 - Experience with Microsoft Ads is a plus
   

 - Strong understanding of paid search fundamentals including keywords, bidding strategies, and account structure
   

 - Experience analyzing performance using Google Analytics or GA4
   

 - Experience using AI tools to support ad copy creation, analysis, or workflow efficiency
   


SOFT SKILLS

 - Strong organizational and prioritization skills across multiple accounts
   

 - Analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into clear insights
   

 - Clear written and verbal communication skills
   

 - High attention to detail with proactive issue identification
   

 - Comfortable working in fast-moving, client-facing environments
   


LANGUAGE & LOCATION

 - English level: C1 or C2 required
   

 - Location: Latin America (remote)
   

 - Availability: U.S. business hours required
   


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience supporting high-spend or multi-location paid search accounts
   

 - Exposure to CRO, landing page testing, or conversion tracking
   

 - Familiarity with ClickUp or similar project management tools




SUCCESS METRICS (FIRST 3 TO 6 MONTHS)

 - Independently manage and optimize assigned paid search campaigns
   

 - Deliver accurate, insight-driven performance reports on time
   

 - Identify and execute optimizations that improve efficiency or conversions
   

 - Build trust with strategists and clients through consistent, high-quality work
   

 - Show clear progression toward a Paid Media Strategist skill set
   


COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Competitive compensati

source_jobs.title: Director of Trust & Safety
source_jobs.description:
This is a net-new leadership role for a peer-to-peer marketplace scaling fast, built to give Trust & Safety a real seat at the table instead of being a reactive cost center. You'll report straight to the COO and own the function end to end: fraud prevention, identity verification, investigations, and moderation. Success looks like a measurably safer platform within a year, fewer fraud losses, faster incident response, and a T&S team running on real workflows and automation instead of tribal knowledge.

What You'll Own

 - Build and run the Trust & Safety operating model from the ground up: team structure, workflows, tooling, and the roadmap to scale it

 - Drive down fraud losses and platform risk by defining and tracking the metrics that actually move the needle

 - Translate marketplace threats into executive-ready reporting that gets budget and buy-in

 - Partner with Product and Engineering to ship fraud detection, identity verification, and moderation tooling that delivers measurable results, not just features

 - Own high-severity incident response, connecting Legal, Engineering, and Operations so investigations actually change policy and product

 - Evaluate and manage vendor relationships for fraud and identity tools, owning the build-vs-buy calls

 - Set the policy foundation with Legal, keeping it audit-ready and enforceable across the org

What We're Looking For

 - 7+ years in Trust & Safety, Fraud Operations, or Risk Management

 - 5+ years leading people, including managing other managers or senior ICs

 - A track record of building or scaling a Trust & Safety, fraud, or marketplace-integrity function, not just running an existing one

 - Proven ability to turn operational problems into systems and influence cross-functional roadmaps

 - Experience reporting metrics and outcomes directly to executive stakeholders

 - Background in a high-growth marketplace, platform, or consumer internet business

 - Professional fluency in English, C1 minimum, written and spoken

Nice to Have

 - Background in P2P marketplaces, rental platforms, or fintech

 - Hands-on experience with identity verification or fraud tools like Jumio, LexisNexis, TransUnion, or IPQS

 - Experience standing up AI or automation-driven workflows inside a T&S org

Tools and Stack
Zendesk, Snowflake, Jira, Confluence, Slack, plus identity/fraud platforms like Jumio or IPQS

Details

 - Location: Remote, Colombia

 - Time Zone: CT

 - Type: Full-time

About the Company
An established, fast-scaling two-sided marketplace in flexible housing, connecting hundreds of thousands of property owners with renters needing month-plus stays. Profitable, over a decade in business, and recently recognized as a category leader in its space.

source_jobs.title: Senior AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity

A well-funded AI company in the healthcare infrastructure space is building the engine that automates some of the most complex workflows in health plan operations. This role exists because the product is working and the team needs a senior engineer who can own significant parts of the AI architecture independently. In the first year, you will go from shipping production pipelines to being the person who defines how the company builds LLM-powered systems at scale.

What You'll Own

 - Architect and ship LLM-powered pipelines that automate complex multi-step clinical decision workflows end-to-end

 - Build and maintain back-end services in Python that integrate directly with enterprise health systems (FHIR, HL7, EDI)

 - Own the quality and reliability of AI outputs through evaluation frameworks, structured outputs, and production monitoring

 - Drive integrations with external enterprise environments, handling compliance constraints and audit requirements that come with healthcare data

 - Translate requirements from product and customer success teams into scoped technical solutions, working directly with non-technical stakeholders when needed

 - Operate across the stack from RAG and document pipelines through to cloud infrastructure and observability tooling

What We're Looking For

 - 5+ years of software engineering experience, with at least 2 years shipping AI/ML systems in production (not research or internal tooling)

 - Hands-on experience building and deploying LLM applications: prompt engineering, RAG pipelines, fine-tuning, eval harnesses

 - Strong Python back-end skills: FastAPI, async patterns, typing, pytest

 - Experience with cloud infrastructure on AWS, GCP, or Azure

 - Proven ability to work with enterprise integration requirements including compliance, auditability, and security constraints

 - Comfortable scoping and communicating technical solutions directly with clients or non-technical stakeholders

Nice to Have

 - Prior work in healthcare systems or HIPAA-governed environments

 - Experience with FHIR, HL7, or EDI data formats

 - TypeScript, Next.js, or front-end exposure

 - Monorepo experience at scale

Tools and Stack

Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Temporal, Redis, AWS (RDS, S3, Bedrock, IAM), Kubernetes, Terraform, Docker, GitHub Actions, Pydantic AI, OpenAI, Google Gemini, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Grafana, Sentry, Logfire, PostHog

Details

 - Location: Remote, Latin America

 - Time Zone: US overlap expected (confirm window with client)

 - Salary: Competitive — share your expectations when you apply.

 - Type: Full-time

 - Start: ASAP

About the Company

An early-stage AI company automating utilization management and clinical review workflows for health plans, TPAs, and medical management companies. They integrate directly into existing enterprise systems and operate in a regulated environment where accuracy and auditability are non-negotiable. Small, focused team building infrastructure that is already in production at real clients.

How It Works:

 1. Apply below — takes 2 minutes.

 2. Within 5 minutes of applying, you'll receive a phone call from our AI hiring assistant for a quick screening.

 3. If you pass the screening, you'll move into an interview with a LatamCent recruiter.

 4. Top candidates are presented to our client for their interview process.

Top engineering careers https://latamcent.com/careers Provided by LatamCent.

notes: Mixed roles with a focus on tech and sales. High startup likelihood due to rapid hiring and growth.
